data.title: Hisense Celebrates Kick-off of FIFA Club World Cup™ as Official Partner, Marking New Milestone in Global Sports Journey
data.description:
Hisense Celebrates Kick-off of FIFA Club World Cup™ as Official Partner, Marking New Milestone in Global Sports Journey

Hisense Celebrates Kick-off of FIFA Club World Cup™ as Official Partner, Marking New Milestone in Global Sports Journey

PR Newswire

QINGDAO, China, June 14, 2025

QINGDAO, China, June 14, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Hisense, a leading brand in global consumer electronics and home appliances, celebrates the opening of the FIFA Club World Cup 2025™ as an Official Partner, reinforcing its long-standing commitment to global sports. For Hisense, every match is not just a competition, but an opportunity to inspire, connect, and lead through technology.

As an Official Sponsor of the FIFA World Cup™ in 2018 and 2022, and Official Partner of UEFA EURO 2016™, 2020™, and 2024™, Hisense continues to strengthen its association with the world's most prestigious tournaments. The brand has further expanded its influence through high-profile partnerships in other fields, including the PSG, NBA (North America), NRL (within Australia), Real Madrid (MEA & Spain), NASCAR (USA), Red Bull Racing in Formula 1, and leading organizations in esports and football.

For fans watching from home, Hisense is redefining the matchday experience. According to Omdia, Hisense has led the global market for 100-inch and above TVs with a 56.7% volume share in 2023, 2024, and Q1 2025, delivering ultra-large screens that recreate the stadium atmosphere in living rooms worldwide. Every pass, goal, and celebration come to life with immersive clarity, making fans feel part of the action.

Looking ahead, Hisense remains committed to its brand mission of enriching people's lives through innovative technology. By deepening its presence across diverse sports and entertainment platforms, Hisense strives to create unforgettable moments that connect fans worldwide. Through continuous innovation in display and audio technologies, the brand aims to inspire passion, foster unity, and empower every viewer to truly "Own the Moment."

Through its powerful synergy of sports partnerships and display innovation, Hisense continues to help fans everywhere "Own the Moment" and enjoy the excitement of competition—wherever they are.

About Hisense

Hisense, founded in 1969, is a globally recognized leader in home appliances and consumer electronics with operations in over 160 countries, specializing in delivering high-quality multimedia products, home appliances, and intelligent IT solutions. According to Omdia, Hisense ranks No. 2 worldwide in total TV volume shipments (2022-2024) and No. 1 globally in the 100-inch and over TV segment (2023- Q12025). As the first official partner of the FIFA Club World Cup 2025™, Hisense is committed to global sports partnerships as a way to connect with audiences worldwide.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/hisense-celebrates-kick-off-of-fifa-club-world-cup-as-official-partner-marking-new-milestone-in-global-sports-journey-302481769.html

SOURCE Hisense

data.title: Prix Fitzgerald 2025 Laureate Announced at Hôtel Belles Rives
data.description:
CAP D’ANTIBES, France, June 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- During a celebrated ceremony at the iconic Hôtel Belles Rives in Juan-les-Pins on the French Riviera, the jury of the 2025 Prix Fitzgerald announced its latest laureate: Pulitzer Prize–winning author Richard Ford, recognized for his poignant novel Be Mine (Le paradis des fous, published in France by Éditions de l’Olivier).

A Media Snippet accompanying this announcement is available in this link.

The Prix Fitzgerald, created in 2011 by Marianne Estène-Chauvin, President of the Francis Scott Fitzgerald Academy and owner of Belles Rives Group, annually honors a work of fiction that embodies the spirit, elegance, and art of living associated with the American literary icon F. Scott Fitzgerald.

This year’s edition holds resonance, marking 100 years since the publication of The Great Gatsby and Fitzgerald’s first stay in Juan-les-Pins in 1925, when he and Zelda moved into Villa Saint-Louis—now Hôtel Belles Rives. It was here, overlooking the same blue expanse of Cap d’Antibes, that Fitzgerald worked on Tender Is the Night, and where the mythos of the Riviera as a playground of writers, artists, and exiles began to crystallize.

Ford’s Be Mine, which revisits his beloved character Frank Bascombe, was praised for its reflection on aging, caregiving, and parenting in America itself, all told with Ford’s signature blend of clarity, wit, and empathy. The jury commended the novel for capturing the existential tensions of modern life with a tone and depth that echoes Fitzgerald’s legacy.

“My gratitude to the Prix Fitzgerald jury for their belief not just in my book but in books in general. We all stand on the shoulders of genius—and being here today at Hôtel Belles Rives, in the year we celebrate the centennial of The Great Gatsby, makes me believe it all possible.”  

The Prix Fitzgerald ceremony was held on the terrace overlooking the romantic, rocky peninsula with its white pier facing the adjacent islands graced by the “green light” that once inspired Fitzgerald’s most famous novel. The celebration concluded with a Riviera-style dinner held on the legendary terraces of this historic landmark.

“Richard Ford’s Be Mine is a masterful—and rare—lesson in restraint, humanity, and emotional clarity. Ford dares to do what few still allow themselves: to write quietly, without flourish, about the subtle pain of fading feelings, the dignity of ordinary lives, and fidelity as an elegant form of melancholy. No grand gestures. No theatrics. Just clean, direct, implacable—and profoundly human—prose. A clarity without drama, yet marked by a light gravity that strikes with precision. It’s a novel that doesn’t try to shine—and that’s precisely why it does,” said Marianne Estène-Chauvin, President of the Francis Scott Fitzgerald Academy and owner of Groupe Belles Rives.

“Like Fitzgerald,” she continued, “Ford understands that the deepest truths live in silences, in hesitations, in sideways glances. He carries the elegance of disenchantment, the rejection of sentimentality—a style that never needs to raise its voice to move us. It is an honor—and, indeed, a distinctly Fitzgeraldian kind of jubilation—to welcome him among the Prix Fitzgerald laureates.”

Ford joins an esteemed list of past American laureates including Joyce Carol Oates (2024), Quentin Tarantino (2023), Jonathan Dee (2022), Jeffrey Eugenides (2019), Jay McInerney (2016), and Christopher Bollen (2015). Special distinctions have also included the Gatsby Prize to Thadée Klossowski de Rola and the Zelda Prize to Dominique Bona.

Each year, a jury of writers, journalists, and cultural figures—presided over by Bertrand de Saint Vincent, Deputy Director of Le Figaro—selects the Prix Fitzgerald recipient. Nominations are revealed in March, with finalists determined by mid-May, ahead of the June ceremony.

The Prix Fitzgerald remains a singular literary honor on the international scene, not only celebrating literary excellence but also perpetuating the mythos and joie de vivre of the Jazz Age on the Riviera—now more poignant than ever in this centennial year of The Great Gatsby.

For more information, visit bellesrives.com and follow on Instagram @bellesrives.

ABOUT HÔTEL BELLES RIVES
Hôtel Belles Rives is an Art Deco gem that stands above the sparkling blue waters of the Mediterranean. F. Scott Fitzgerald penned Tender is the Night here, and the property is a testament to the breezy extravagance of a bygone era yet reveals a modern design, offering 43 recently renovated rooms and suites. A gastronomic Mediterranean experience awaits at La Passagère, the onsite, one-star Michelin restaurant led by culinary maestro, Aurélien Véquaud. Classic cocktails can be found at the newly renovated Bar Fitzgerald, named for the property’s original resident. The hotel’s private beach features the Belles Rives Beach Restaurant, the Water Sports Club, and the jetty that’s home to the hotel’s private boat dock. Extend that sun kissed, blissed-out feeling with an innovative treatment by luxe Swiss skincare house, Valmont onsite at its namesake beauty corner. The property—with an ownership bloodline dating back to the 1930s—is now helmed by the fourth generation, the 43-year-old, Antoine Chauvin-Estène who is imparting an egalitarian and refreshing approach to this emblematic riviera classic.

MEDIA CONTACTS:
Nadeige Martelly, AMPR Global
nadeige@andriamitsakospr.com
+1 786 863 1363

Andria Mitsakos, AMPR Global
andria@andriamitsakospr.com
+1 954 294 4710

data.title: Best Gold IRA Companies 2025 Announced: Top Gold IRA Rollover Companies Ranked
data.description:

Visit Augusta Precious Metals and Get Their Free Kit

Unlike other top 5 gold IRA companies that spread themselves thin with cryptocurrencies or traditional IRAs, Augusta specializes in precious metals exclusively. Their track record speaks volumes - not a single complaint on BBB and BCA since they started. Hundreds of verified customers give them stellar ratings: 4.8/5 on Trustpilot and 4.9/5 on Google.

The company's education-first approach stands out in Augusta Precious Metals reviews. New clients get a free one-on-one web conference with Harvard-trained economist Devlyn Steele. They also receive amazing perks: a free gold welcome gift for new accounts, up to 10 years of covered storage fees, and a 100% satisfaction guarantee with a 7-day money-back policy.

Looking to add precious metals to your retirement portfolio? Here's why most investors make Augusta their top IRA gold choice.

Check Out Why IRAEmpire Recommends Augusta Precious Metals

Alternatively, read the full list of the best gold IRA companies of 2025.

What Is Augusta Precious Metals and Why It Stands Out

Augusta Precious Metals specializes in precious metals IRAs. The company stands out by focusing solely on gold and silver, unlike competitors who spread themselves across investment types of all sizes.

Founded in 2012: A Focus on Gold and Silver IRAs

Isaac Nuriani's 11-year old Augusta Precious Metals started in 2012 with a clear goal: to enable Americans to vary their retirement savings through physical precious metals. This Beverly Hills, California-based family business has shown steadfast dedication to gold and silver investments for over a decade. The company helps people protect their wealth against economic uncertainty and market volatility through tangible assets.

Augusta sets itself apart from other precious metals companies through its steadfast dedication to their specialty. The company has mastered gold and silver investments while competitors branched into cryptocurrency or traditional IRAs. This focused strategy lets them provide deeper expertise, better client experiences, and detailed support throughout your investment.

Specialization vs. Diversification: Why It Matters

Augusta's specialized approach has created remarkable results. The company has managed to keep a spotless reputation since 2012, with zero complaints filed with both the Better Business Bureau and Business Consumer Alliance. This perfect record earned them an A+ rating from the BBB and a prestigious AAA rating from the BCA.

Augusta's customer satisfaction ratings shine on multiple platforms:

  • TrustLink: Perfect 5.0 rating based on 288 reviews

  • Google My Business: 4.9 rating from 493 reviews

  • Consumer Affairs: 5.0 rating based on 141 reviews

  • Facebook: 96% rating from 88 reviews

The company has also earned many industry awards, including Stevie Awards for Customer Service Success and Sales Excellence. Money magazine named them "Best Overall Gold IRA Company" for multiple consecutive years because of this exceptional performance.

Augusta builds lifelong partnerships rather than just selling precious metals. Their dedicated customer success agents stay with clients from their first purchase through potential distributions and beyond. This personal touch sets them apart from competitors who might treat you as just another transaction.

Who Should Consider Augusta?

Serious long-term investors with established retirement savings will benefit most from Augusta Precious Metals. The company serves these clients best:

People who value detailed learning resources before investing decisions. Augusta offers free gold IRA guides, weekly market updates, and a vast video library covering Federal Reserve operations to retirement risk management.

Investors wanting expert guidance without paperwork headaches benefit from Augusta's optimized approach. The company handles about 95% of gold IRA documentation. This makes switching from traditional retirement accounts simple.

People looking for stability during market turmoil appreciate Augusta's track record of staying steady compared to traditional investments, especially during the 2022 market downturn.

Augusta's education-first approach and specialized expertise have made them one of America's highest-rated gold IRA providers. Their success shows why countless investors trust them with precious metals investments.

Get a Free Gold IRA Kit from Augusta Precious Metals Here

How Augusta Precious Metals Simplifies Gold IRA Setup

Setting up a Gold IRA might seem complicated, but Augusta Precious Metals has created an efficient four-step process that removes the complexity. Augusta's approach to simplification stands out among top 5 gold IRA companies when you look at best gold IRA company reviews.

Step 1: Open a Self-Directed IRA

The process starts when Augusta assigns you a personal customer success agent who becomes your dedicated point of contact. This representative helps you complete the paperwork to set up your self-directed IRA account. Augusta handles about 95% of the paperwork for you, unlike other companies that might overwhelm you with forms.

Your agent works with Augusta's preferred custodian, Equity Trust. They bring over 30 years of experience and manage more than $25 billion in assets. This partnership will give you a correctly established account from the start and a solid foundation for your best gold IRA investments.

Step 2: Rollover or Transfer Retirement Funds

Augusta's IRA processing team helps you fund your new self-directed IRA. Most clients move their funds from existing retirement accounts such as traditional IRAs, Roth IRAs, 401(k)s, or 403(b)s. You can maintain your tax advantages while varying your portfolio with this approach.

Your current retirement account custodian works directly with the team who manages all communication and documentation. You save time and avoid stress with this hands-off approach. The IRS allows 60 days to complete rollovers without tax consequences, but Augusta's efficiency ensures quick transitions well within this timeframe.

Step 3: Choose IRS-Approved Gold or Silver

Augusta's order desk guides you through selecting IRA-eligible precious metals once your account has funds. Their product specialists present all available options without pressure. You can build a diverse portfolio based on your financial goals.

The IRS has specific requirements for precious metals in IRAs. Augusta offers several approved products:

Augusta's education-first approach shines here. They teach rather than sell, so you can make informed decisions about your best rated gold IRA selections.

Visit Augusta Precious Metals and Get Their Free Gold IRA Kit

Step 4: Secure Storage or Home Delivery Options

Your precious metals go to an IRS-approved depository of your choice with free shipping and insurance. Augusta partners with the Delaware Depository. They offer $1 billion in all-risk insurance through London underwriters and maintain top-security facilities.

Storage facilities are available in many locations across the United States. You can find them in Los Angeles, Salt Lake City, Las Vegas, New York, and several other cities. This nationwide network lets you pick a location that works best for you.

Augusta offers secure home delivery for non-IRA precious metals purchases. You can also work with your custodian to arrange visits to view your metals or pick them up as part of a distribution.

Clients consistently praise this straightforward process in Augusta precious metals reviews. The company's dedication to simplifying what could be a complex process shows why they remain among the most trusted names in the industry. Their approach makes it easier for investors to vary their retirement portfolios with precious metals.

Education-First Approach: Augusta's Biggest Differentiator

Augusta stands out from other gold IRA companies because of its educational philosophy. The company focuses on making you a knowledgeable investor before discussing any products.

Augusta's educational approach centers on their exclusive web conference with Devlyn Steele, the company's Director of Education. He brings over 45 years of financial experience as a Harvard-trained economist and offers guidance that few others in the industry can match. The conference's value comes from its pressure-free environment. You won't sit through a sales pitch - the session focuses on education. You can ask questions about inflation protection, portfolio diversification, and wealth preservation. Many reviews point to this conference as the key reason people choose Augusta over other top 5 gold IRA companies.

Get The Free One-on-One Web Conference Here

Weekly Market Updates and Video Library

After your first conference, Augusta keeps you updated through regular market communications. These weekly updates explain economic trends, precious metals performance, and factors that could affect your gold IRA investments.

The company also maintains a rich video library that covers:

  • Economic factors affecting gold and silver prices

  • Tax considerations for precious metals IRAs

These educational resources help you stay informed about market developments that could affect your investments. This steady communication has helped Augusta become the best-rated gold IRA provider.

Gold IRA Guide and Scam Awareness Resources

Augusta shows its dedication to education through detailed written materials. Their Gold IRA Guide explains every aspect of precious metals investing - from simple concepts to advanced strategies. This handbook serves as your reference throughout your investment experience.

The company also takes an unusual step by teaching clients about industry pitfalls. Their Scam Awareness Guide reveals common tactics that less reputable companies use. This knowledge helps you make smart decisions no matter which provider you choose.

This transparent approach explains why many investors turn to Augusta for their IRA gold needs. The company builds trust by putting knowledge first. This approach sets Augusta apart from other companies that focus mainly on sales. Augusta aims to create confident investors who understand why they're adding precious metals to their portfolio.

Get Their Scam Awareness Guide Here

Ryan says, "I found their free kit extremely helpful and I've read almost every free gold IRA kit present out there."

Customer Experience: Why Gold IRA Investors Choose Augusta

Augusta Precious Metals stands out with their amazing customer experience, which explains why investors pick them over competitors. The company builds relationships with clients from day one and continues this approach throughout their investment experience.

Lifetime Account Support from Dedicated Agents

New Augusta clients receive their own customer success agent who becomes their main contact point. This dedicated professional stays available throughout their account's lifetime-one of the most important advantages over competitors who often vanish after the first sale. The agent answers simple questions about precious metals and links clients with specialized departments whenever needed.

Augusta's lifetime support goes beyond simple service and includes:

  • Help with required minimum distributions (RMDs) and other paperwork

  • Support with additional precious metals purchases

  • Assistance for buyback processes during liquidation

No-Pressure Sales and Transparent Communication

Augusta sets itself apart with a refreshing no-commission sales model. The team takes time to understand each client's financial situation before making personalized recommendations. Clients often say this approach "feels more like working with a financial advisor than a salesperson".

Clients consistently praise Augusta's clear communication style. Reviews show that representatives are "extremely knowledgeable when it comes to helping select specific gold and silver products" and "take whatever time is necessary to explain everything".

Zero Complaints on BBB and BCA Since 2012

Augusta managed to keep a perfect record of zero complaints with both the Better Business Bureau and Business Consumer Alliance since 2012. This clean record helped them earn an A+ BBB rating and AAA rating from BCA.

The company's steadfast dedication to excellence got them two prestigious Stevie Awards for Customer Service Success and Sales.

Real User Testimonials and Success Stories

Verified customers give Augusta near-perfect ratings across review platforms:

  • "The idea of rolling over my 401K retirement money into a gold IRA was intimidating, but the professional people at Augusta made it very simple"

  • "Their staff is extremely professional and I would feel comfortable using them for such an important decision"

  • "They are especially knowledgeable when it came to helping me select specific gold and silver products to meet my investment requirements"

Reviews often highlight how Augusta's representatives stay patient, thorough, and educational instead of pushy-exactly what investors want when making big financial decisions.

Exclusive Benefits That Set Augusta Apart

Augusta Precious Metals stands out among the best gold IRA companies by offering exclusive perks beyond their exceptional service and education. These benefits add real value to your investment experience.

New gold IRA accounts with Augusta come with a complimentary gold welcome gift. You can choose from three different gold coins. This benefit adds precious metals value to your portfolio from day one and enhances your investment right from the start.

Qualifying customers don't pay custodian, storage, and setup costs for up to 10 years. Research suggests Augusta might be the only company in the industry that offers this extensive benefit. This advantage lets you focus on growing your investment without worrying about ongoing expenses.

Augusta's "Highest Buyback Guarantee" sets them apart from competitors. Unlike other companies that make selling metals difficult, Augusta lets you reclaim your investment easily. You can cancel your buyback transaction if you get a higher offer elsewhere within 24 hours. 100% Satisfaction and 7-Day Money-Back Guarantee

Augusta shows their confidence through two powerful guarantees. Their 100% Satisfaction Guarantee keeps you in control and lets you switch to other providers if needed. They also offer a 7-Day Money-Back Guarantee on qualified purchases. You can open your account with complete peace of mind. These guarantees showcase Augusta's steadfast dedication to customer satisfaction.

Best Gold IRA Companies 2025: Conclusion

Augusta Precious Metals has earned the trust of investors who manage their precious metals portfolios with them. Their education-first philosophy sets them apart from competitors, particularly through their exclusive one-on-one web conference with Harvard-trained economist Devlyn Steele. This steadfast dedication to creating knowledgeable investors rather than just customers forms the foundations for lasting relationships.

The company's four-step process takes the complexity out of precious metals investing. What seems daunting becomes remarkably straightforward. The most compelling proof of Augusta's outstanding service shows in their flawless record. Augusta Precious Metals delivers exactly what retirement investors need: stability, transparency, and expertise. Market volatility and economic uncertainty might increase, but Augusta strengthens your portfolio with tangible precious metals assets. Serious investors looking to protect retirement wealth through diversification will find Augusta Precious Metals the gold standard in 2025 and beyond.

Each client is assigned a dedicated customer success agent to provide personalized support throughout the process.

Q3. What kind of customer support does Augusta Precious Metals offer? Augusta provides lifetime account support from dedicated agents who stay with you from your initial purchase through potential distributions and beyond. Q5. What types of precious metals can I invest in through Augusta? data.title: 'I'm 68 and my 401(k) has dwindled to $82,000': My husband committed financial infidelity and has $50,000 in credit-card debt. What now?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'I have $3,000 in gold and Social Security income for me and my spouse makes $46,180 a year'

Dear Quentin,

Without going into details of my spouse's financial infidelity, I would like your opinion. Here is the bottom line. I'm 68 and my 401(k) has dwindled to $82,000. I have $3,000 in gold and Social Security income for me and my spouse totals $46,180 a year.

Our home is paid off and the estimated value is somewhere between $600,000 and $1 million. We live in a vacation area. Many out-of-state folks have moved in and the price of even a tiny home is outrageous right now. Yearly land taxes at $5,000.

Our adult children owe us a total of $90,000 and are attempting monthly payments of various amounts. My spouse has $50,000 in credit-card debt. I abhor any debt. What is the smartest way to pay off this debt?

Feeling Desperate

Related: My mother-in-law thought the world's richest man needed Apple gift cards. How on Earth could she fall for this scam?

Dear Desperate,

Financial infidelity - keeping secrets like excessive spending a secret - can be as damaging as more traditional infidelity.

Your children could pay off your credit-card debt, almost twice over, if they were able to stick to a payment plan. But lending money to people - children, friends, neighbors, relatives - who have gotten themselves into the red won't necessarily solve their problem. It will merely create a problem for more parties: the lender, who wonders why the money was never repaid in a years-long game of cat-and-mouse, and the borrower, who has added creditor to their list.

The smartest way to pay off your debt is to write all your expenses in one column and your income in another and create your own personal Department of Good Housekeeping. Slash and burn and pay off that $50,000 at all costs. Your husband should also prioritize his credit-card debt before you do anything - including eating out, going to the movies or the theatre, buying new sneakers (even if they're on sale), or taking a vacation.

The smartest way to pay off your debt is to create your own personal Department of Good Housekeeping: pay off that $50,000 at all costs.

You don't mention the cause of your husband's financial infidelity, but unless you deal with this first and foremost, the chances of it happening again are high and/or probable. If he has a gambling problem or a substance misuse issue, for instance, it won't go away even if you do pay off the debt. Paying off the debt could even provide him with a new impetus to repeat the errors of the past. If this $50,000 debt was news to you, this is a separate problem.

That said, your priority is to pay off your debt on a regular basis, automating those payments, with a medium- to long-term goal of getting back on track. The National Foundation for Credit Counseling is a nonprofit organization that can help you and your husband put together a budget and a realistic plan to pay off your debt. The American Consumer Credit Counseling is another nonprofit organization that helps people in your situation.

Don't miss: 'I have committed financial infidelity': I racked up $50,000 in debt to help my troubled son - and have not told my husband. How do I get out of this mess?

Negotiating with credit-card companies

You could also attempt to renegotiate the debt with the credit-card companies. "Call your credit-card company and ask to speak with the debt-settlement, loss-mitigation or hardship department," Bankrate.com advises. "A general customer-service representative won't have the authority to approve your request. Once you're connected with someone who has the ability to negotiate with you, explain your situation and make your offer. Be polite but firm."

"Outline your terms," Bankrate says. "If you're considering filing bankruptcy or hiring a professional to help you with your debt, let the card issuer know and mention that you'd rather work things out directly. At this point, be prepared for the card issuer to potentially freeze your credit limit or close your account." Beware of for-profit debt-settlement companies, which frequently end up costing you more money for a less-than-satisfactory outcome.

You are sitting on a lot of equity, so you have another choice to make: do you take this moment to review your finances and downsize.

There are two main methods of paying off debt: the snowball method (paying off the card with the lowest amount on it first) and the avalanche method (paying off the debt with the highest interest rate first). The first is a way to help motivate people to get out of the red, but paying down the highest rate first makes the most sense to me. Your decision is whether these payments come out of your husband's income or joint funds.

Looking ahead, you are sitting on a lot of equity, so you have another choice to make: Do you take this moment to review your finances, downsize, pay off your husband's credit-card debt and provide yourself with a cash cushion in more modest surroundings? Can you trust your husband with a cash cushion in a joint account? My biggest concern for you is that, after you pay off this debt, your husband will repeat the mistakes of the past.

Related: I have $1,000 in credit-card debt. Will I be able to hide my inheritance from the bank?

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com, and follow Quentin Fottrell on X, the platform formerly known as Twitter.

The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

More columns from Quentin Fottrell:

I met a friend for lunch. When the check arrived, she said, 'Thank you so much for paying!' Was I taken for a fool?

'I once felt that I had nothing and I was nothing': I had a secret $8,000 debt that I was afraid to reveal to my boyfriend, but I turned my life around

My father died, leaving everything to my 90-year-old stepmother. Do I have a right to ask her if I'm in her will?

Check out The Moneyist's private Facebook group, where members help answer life's thorniest money issues. Post your questions, or weigh in on the latest Moneyist columns.

By emailing your questions to The Moneyist or posting your dilemmas on The Moneyist Facebook group, you agree to have them published anonymously on MarketWatch.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms, including via third parties.

-Quentin Fottrell

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  06-14-25 1850ET

data.title: 'It might be another Apple or Microsoft': My wife invested $100K in one stock and it exploded 1,500%. Do we sell?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'She shops with coupons at TJ Maxx'

Dear Quentin,

We are novice investors, limited to 401(k)s and index funds that we manage ourselves. However, we took a chance on a stock shortly after its IPO. It has exploded, up more than 15 times. Not 15% - 15 times. That is, 1,500%. We're the dog that caught the car.

We bought the technology company Palantir Technologies at around $9 per share. My wife did it on a hunch from watching an item on CNBC. I nearly collapsed when she told me about how much it went up. She shops with coupons at TJ Maxx.

Now what? It might be another Apple (AAPL) or Microsoft (MSFT), great for life. We don't need the money, but we can't see down the road to know how to decide when to sell and how much to sell. Maybe we never sell and we pass it onto our kids (we're 70, with long-life genes)?

If we had just doubled our money, the easy thing to do would be to sell half and play with house money. But 15 times? This is the kind of dilemma we never expected to be faced with at this time in our lives. What should we do now?

Lucky Picker

Related: 'God works in mysterious ways': I became a Nvidia millionaire playing 'World of Warcraft.' Am I smart - or just lucky?

Dear Lucky,

There are no immediate reasons to sell.

You said it: You got lucky, real lucky. Congratulations. All the caveats and warnings that would have come with investing $100,000 in one stock did not bear poison fruit. Instead, you have become septuagenarian surfers riding the wave of the AI boom.

You are in your 70s, so your first priority should be to enjoy your good fortune, and your second priority (as much as there is one) can be leaving shares to your children. If you don't have a home to pay off, what else would improve your quality of life right now?

The company's own projections are rosy and, while no one can predict the future, you might kick yourself in five years if you sell now. But remember: There are no guarantees. If there are trips or house renovations or anything that can improve your life now, do it.

Sometimes, the biggest benefit from receiving a windfall or having a lucky break on the stock market is to enjoy the peace of mind that brings. So you are already enjoying the neverending porridge pot of nourishment that such good fortune brings by simply living your life.

Palantir - which provides software to the military, defense and corporate sectors - has its many cheerleaders and, yes, its detractors, who argue that the stock is overvalued at current levels. Some have even asked whether it's Wall Street's biggest "stock bubble" of 2025.

Palantir's artificial-intelligence boom

Palantir, a software company that powers big-data analytics and has benefited from the revolution in AI technology in recent years, could eventually have a market capitalization of $1 trillion, according to Wedbush analyst Dan Ives. It currently has a market cap of $300 billion.

"Palantir's (PLTR) unique AI software approach will be a positive growth catalyst as governments are to increase efficiency with more software/lower headcount," Wedbush analysts wrote recently. Ives has an "outperform" rating and $140 price target on the stock.

There are risks, of course. Among them: More than half of Palantir's revenue comes from government contracts, which is not an insignificant concern given the Trump administration's DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency) initiatives.

Still, retail investors seem to have taken to Palantir Chief Executive Officer Alex Karp, who has a knack for soundbites and, after all, is presiding over a success story for investors, so much so that many analysts say the stock has priced in a chunk of future earnings.

"Palantir is on fire," Karp said on the company's first-quarter earnings call last month. Palantir upped its targets for full-year revenue and operating income. Analysts tracked by FactSet expect its adjusted EPS growth rate to slow from 62.5% in the latest quarter to 46.1% in the June quarter.

Analyzing a stock's performance

Some analysts are divided on Palantir. Morningstar analyst Mark Giarelli recently raised his fair-value estimate on the stock to $100 from $90, citing increasing demand from government and commercial customers for the company's optimization software.

Jefferies analyst Brent Thill, who had a "sell" rating on Palantir stock last year, with a $28 price target, suggesting a 55% downside potential at the time, has an "underperform" rating on the stock, citing the company's high valuation multiples and large sales of shares by insiders.

Analysts have all sorts of methods for choosing stocks - including fundamental analysis or technical analysis - but there are no guarantees for anyone, even for those who have backed Palantir's success thus far. And history is replete with bad calls.

A stock's performance versus its current share price is always in focus. "Investors have traditionally used fundamental analysis for longer-term trades, relying on metrics like earnings per share, price-to-earnings ratio, P/E growth and dividend yield," says Charles Schwab.

"Technical analysis, on the other hand, bypasses the underlying company's fundamentals and instead looks for statistical patterns on stock charts that might foretell future price moves," it adds, assuming the stock reflects all the publicly available information about a company.

Navigating your own uncertainty

Some investors, particularly retail investors, might take some profit now or even take their chips off the table entirely, and weigh up the risk/reward factor, and decide that they have enough money to enjoy their life regardless of the stock's future performance.

That would come down to our comfort level navigating uncertainty. We live in turbulent political and economic times, from President Donald Trump's trade war to an actual war in the Ukraine and slowing economic growth in the U.S.

Over the past 100 years, U.S. economists have repeated the hubris that preceded economic disasters and stock-market crashes of Oct. 28, 1929; Oct. 19, 1987; and Sept. 15, 2008. Having a realistic outlook about all eventualities is not an unreasonable position to have.

You don't need the money. Just be aware that your good fortune is concentrated in one stock. Apple, the first company to hit a $1 trillion market cap, has maintained its unique hardware, software, services and brand. Palantir appears to have the world at its feet, for now.

Keep shopping with TJ Maxx coupons, and hold onto your shares. Investing, like shopping, is part return on your investment and value for money, and part finding that bargain. It's the hope that we will continue to improve our quality of life that keeps us coming back.

Related: 'I'm convinced the U.S. will be drawn into World War III': How do I prepare my finances?

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com, and follow Quentin Fottrell on X, the platform formerly known as Twitter.

The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

My father-in-law has dementia and is moving in with us. Can we invoice him for a caregiver?

I have $1,000 in credit-card debt. Will I be able to hide my inheritance from the bank?

'I prepaid our mom's rent for a year': My sister is a millionaire and never helps our mother. How do I cut her out of her will?

data.title: My friend wants me to join in a political protest. I'm worried about my job. Am I a coward if I say no?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'Some of our firm's clients have a political and economic stake in the issues raised by my friend'

Dear Quentin,

A good friend made politically-charged posters using AI and wants me to spread them on my social media. She works for herself in communications and is a very successful and vocal activist in her own right. I'm reluctant to post these posters, although the AI images are truly amazing. They look like they could be hanging in the Smithsonian.

She has wanted me to march with her and protest for various issues. I have not marched with her, as I work for a consultancy firm and some of our firm's clients have a political and economic stake in the issues raised by my friend. Should I share my friend's AI posters on my social media to show her support, and just restrict who can see them?

My work takes me to LA, D.C., Florida and New York.

Am I a coward if I say no?

An Engaged Citizen

Related: My mother-in-law thought the world's richest man needed Apple gift cards. How on Earth could she fall for this scam?

Dear Engaged,

Regardless of your political affiliations, whether you support the current administration or not, it's important to stand up for what you believe in and have your voice heard. You can do that on social media, by walking the streets with a placard - or at the ballot box. But only one of those three activities is private, and only one will not put your job or livelihood at risk. Your friend may have less at stake by sharing these AI-generated posters. Your manager could decide that your activism is against the terms of your employment contract and/or puts accounts at risk.

Similar to your job, journalists in legacy news organizations are typically not permitted to participate in political protests because they must maintain objectivity and, in the eyes of readers, not be seen as throwing support behind one party over another. Objectivity may not be 100% possible outside of a laboratory, but it's an important goal. Barbara Walters, the late newswoman, never revealed whom she voted for in elections because she regularly interviewed politicians and newsmakers, and aimed to do so without fear or favor.

The First Amendment protects freedom of speech, freedom of the press and freedom to assemble, among other rights. But that applies only to government censorship and federal, state and local government employees. It does not apply to private-sector workers who make their opinions known on issues like Israel, Palestine, immigration protests in Los Angeles, or other political issues closer to home. Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and the Florida Civil Rights Act of 1992 do not include political expression as a protected class.

Related: American companies are getting caught up in ICE raids

Empowerment vs. actual change

Protest can make people feel empowered, even if their actions don't result in change. Your friend wishes to amplify her message. To successfully achieve the latter, it's important to have a specific goal and be clear in what you want to say. The guy who made Tesla (TSLA) bumper stickers, criticizing Tesla CEO Elon Musk's former role as head of the Trump administration's so-called Department of Government Efficiency, did this and reportedly makes $100,000 a month. Yes, protests can be profitable, as well as altruistic.

Other protests are less clear in their purpose and goal: The annual "economic blackout," organized by People's Union USA, which takes place every February, is a noble gesture and a shot across the bow for big retailers who make it tough for the little mom-and-pop to stay in business. It's designed to serve as a black eye for billionaires, but it's a hopelessly confusing campaign that targets consumerism and "big business," and, as far as I understand it, allows people to stay home and watch Netflix (NFLX) or scroll through Facebook (META) instead.

Occupy Wall Street lasted 59 days and gained global attention, and became a political, cultural and social force, although it's debatable whether it achieved its end goals. Still, people actually had to show up and protest in person. Many of those people were, I assume, not working full-time corporate jobs that they were putting at risk. If they did? They would be updating their LinkedIn profiles on day 60. You need to weigh your personal commitments with your commitment to the cause your friend supports rather than your commitment to your friend.

You need to weigh your personal commitments with your commitment to the cause your friend supports rather than your commitment to your friend.

Fisher Phillips, a law firm with offices nationwide, recently issued a warning about workers who wish to engage in public protests. Sharing those posters would be considered political speech. "As political tensions surge and employee expression spills into every corner of the modern workplace, employers are finding themselves caught in the crossfire," it says. "From off-hours protests to heated workplace debates and viral social media posts, the question for us isn't just whether speech is free - but whether it's job-protected."

This applies to wearing T-shirts emblazoned with slogans, and writing and even "liking" posts on social media. State laws vary. "These laws may surprise you, as some prevent employers from terminating the employment of someone who posts even clearly offensive posts unless you can show the communication caused direct business harm," it adds. "Evaluate the workplace impact. Determine whether the post caused operational disruption, reputational damage, or significant internal strife. Absent that, discipline may not be legally or strategically justifiable."

"Employers may not be able to discipline off-duty speech with total impunity. Several states - including California, New York, and Colorado - have statutes protecting employees from retaliation for lawful off-duty conduct; Minnesota, Connecticut, Louisiana, South Carolina, and Wyoming are among the states that specifically protect off-duty political activity," the law firm says. "These laws may prevent employers from firing or disciplining employees for expressing their personal views unless those views have a clear, material impact on the business."

Don't miss: Should I wear my Trump MAGA hat to work?

Make an informed decision

While California is also an employment-at-will state, California Labor Code Section 1101 states that no employer can prevent or forbid an employee from participating in politics or from becoming a candidate for public office. Section 201-D of the New York State Labor Law prohibits discrimination based on "political activities outside of working hours, off of the employer's premises and without the use of the employer's equipment or other property." Again, this state law applies to private-sector workers rather than federal employees.

"If employees are discussing matters that relate to shared working conditions, such as diversity policies, unionization, or workplace safety, their speech may be protected," Fisher Phillips adds. "Discipline in that context could trigger an unfair labor practice charge. The challenge is distinguishing between disruptive or discriminatory conduct, which may be regulated, and protected activity, which may not. If the discussions relate to other protected activity, complaining about discrimination or harassment, fair employment laws might be triggered."

Be honest with your friend. Tell her that you appreciate her passion and energy, but also explain that you don't have the same leeway to share these images given your consultancy work. You're not just putting your own job at risk, but you've also made a commitment to your employer to do the best job possible and uphold the terms of your contract. If do feel compelled to share these protest posters on your social media, by all means do it. Just be aware of the potential risks and consequences before you do so, and make peace with that.

Don't miss: I told my friend to sell his Tesla shares. He did not appreciate my advice. Was I wrong?

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com, and follow Quentin Fottrell on X, the platform formerly known as Twitter.

The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

More columns from Quentin Fottrell:

I'm a Democrat who drives a Tesla. A guy shouted abuse at me for driving a car made by Elon Musk. Should I sell my car?

I met a friend for lunch. When the check arrived, she said, 'Thank you so much for paying!' Was I taken for a fool?

I became a Nvidia millionaire playing 'World of Warcraft.' Am I smart - or just lucky?

-Quentin Fottrell

data.title: Gold Coast Health Plan Denounces Sharing of Medicaid Enrollment Data with Department of Homeland Security
data.description:
Gold Coast Health Plan Denounces Sharing of Medicaid Enrollment Data with Department of Homeland Security

Gold Coast Health Plan Denounces Sharing of Medicaid Enrollment Data with Department of Homeland Security

PR Newswire

CAMARILLO, Calif., June 14, 2025

CAMARILLO, Calif., June 14, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Gold Coast Health Plan (GCHP), the local Medi-Cal plan for Ventura County, is deeply concerned about the possibility of sensitive Medicaid enrollment data—including names, addresses, and immigration statuses—being shared with the Department of Homeland Security as reported by the Associated Press. According to the article, the data sharing was conducted "despite internal objections from Medicaid officials" and without consent from states, health plans, or members.

The data includes information from individuals in various states, including California, that allow those with "unsatisfactory immigration status" to enroll in Medicaid programs funded by state dollars.

"This breach is not just a policy failure—it is a moral one," said Dr. Felix Nuñez, GCHP's chief executive officer. "Trust is the foundation of our health system. Undermining that trust by turning health care into a tool of immigration enforcement jeopardizes the well-being of entire communities. We must protect the right to seek care without fear."

Laura Espinosa, chair of GCHP's governing board, the Ventura County Medi-Cal Managed Care Commission, said news of the data release has heightened the anxieties of a population that is already on edge due to increased activity of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and Border Patrol agents in recent days.

"We stand with our community in denouncing the relentless targeting of our immigrant communities," Espinosa said. "Any use of Medicaid data to further target specific communities will cause irreparable damage."

GCHP became of aware that false rumors were circulating in the community indicating that GCHP had shared the information with ICE.

"Gold Coast Health Plan did not release, and will never release, any private member information to immigration authorities," Dr. Nuñez said. "Any rumor suggesting otherwise is completely false and deeply harmful to the communities we serve."

About Gold Coast Health Plan

Gold Coast Health Plan proudly serves more than 240,000 Medi-Cal members in Ventura County through its network of primary care physicians, specialists, behavioral health providers and hospitals. Since it was founded in 2011, Gold Coast Health Plan has been committed to providing access to high-quality care and improving the health of its members, which include 1 in 3 county residents, 1 in 6 seniors, and 1 in 2 children up to the age of 5. To learn more, visit: www.goldcoasthealthplan.org.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/gold-coast-health-plan-denounces-sharing-of-medicaid-enrollment-data-with-department-of-homeland-security-302481749.html

SOURCE Gold Coast Health Plan

data.title: FinTech Leader Kennel Connection Launches Enhanced Report Cards to Help Pet Care Pros Save Time and Build Trust
data.description:
FinTech Leader Kennel Connection Launches Enhanced Report Cards to Help Pet Care Pros Save Time and Build Trust

Kennel Connection, a leader in pet care management software and a 100GROUP company, is proud to announce a comprehensive update to its Report Cards feature, a powerful and modernized communication tool designed to help pet care businesses deliver personalized updates to clients with ease.

While report cards have long been part of the Kennel Connection experience, this major overhaul introduces a redesigned interface, enhanced functionality, and new delivery options that make the system more powerful and user-friendly than ever.

Built for daycare, boarding, and special services, the revamped Report Cards feature allows pet care facilities to share structured, customized updates via email or SMS in just a few clicks. Staff can quickly add notes about a pet’s behavior, eating habits, elimination, and playtime by selecting from pre-written messages or adding their own touch. The addition of optional pet photos brings each update to life, helping clients feel connected to their pet’s stay while reinforcing trust in the business.

Users can now choose from a variety of sleek, professionally designed templates that reflect the modern pet care experience. Whether you’re aiming for playful and colorful or clean and minimalist, there’s a layout to match your brand and client preferences, offering both visual polish and practical customization.

A Streamlined Workflow with a Personal Touch

With Report Cards, pet care teams can create and send professional, mobile-friendly updates directly from the appointment screen, dashboard, pet card, client card, or schedule. Each report card can be sent once for the full stay or as a daily summary, allowing businesses to tailor the frequency and content to their preferred workflow.

Key capabilities of the revamped Report Cards feature include:

  • Customizable sections for behavior, meals, elimination, and playtime
  • Flexible messaging options including rotating text templates and editable notes
  • Quick delivery via email or text with the option to include pet photos
  • Preview and edit tools for enhanced control and personalization
  • Multiple clean, modern templates to choose from, allowing alignment with business branding

"Client communication is at the heart of any successful pet care operation," said Jeff Brodsly, Owner and CEO of Kennel Connection and 100GROUP. "This new version of Report Cards helps businesses deliver meaningful updates without slowing down their day. It’s about giving pet parents peace of mind while making it easier for teams to stay efficient and consistent."

Part of a Broader Vision

The update of Report Cards aligns with Kennel Connection’s ongoing commitment to innovation, efficiency, and customer satisfaction. As part of its growing suite of tools, including integrated credit card processing, digital contracts, advanced booking, and text-to-pay, Report Cards further supports pet care businesses in delivering a seamless and modern client experience.

About Kennel Connection:

Kennel Connection is a leading provider of innovative software solutions for pet care businesses, offering a comprehensive suite of management tools designed to streamline operations and enhance customer experience. With a focus on empowering pet care professionals, Kennel Connection's feature-rich platform encompasses reservation management, client communication, billing, and reporting, alongside innovative features such as Text-To-Pay, digital contracts, advanced online booking, and integrated credit card processing; all aimed at driving efficiency and organization in day-to-day operations. Kennel Connection leverages cutting-edge technology to deliver tailored solutions that cater to the unique needs of pet care providers nationwide.

For more information about Kennel Connection, visit www.kennelconnection.com.

Erin McCarthy
100GROUP
erin@100group.com
385-955-4307

data.title: FOX NEWS CHANNEL'S BRET BAIER LANDS FIRST INTERVIEW WITH ISRAELI PRIME MINISTER BENJAMIN NETANYAHU SINCE ISRAEL'S STRIKES ON IRAN ON SUNDAY, JUNE 15TH
data.description:

FOX NEWS CHANNEL'S BRET BAIER LANDS FIRST INTERVIEW WITH ISRAELI PRIME MINISTER BENJAMIN NETANYAHU SINCE ISRAEL'S STRIKES ON IRAN ON SUNDAY, JUNE 15TH

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, June 14, 2025

NEW YORK, June 14, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- FOX News Channel's Bret Baier will present a worldwide exclusive live interview with Israeli Prime Minister Bibi Netanyahu on Sunday, June 15th during a special edition of Special Report at 12 PM/ET. This marks Netanyahu's first interview since Israel's attack on Iran's nuclear program and leadership. An encore of the special will also run at 5 PM/ET on Sunday. A transcript will be made available immediately following the interview.

FOX News Channel (FNC) is a 24-hour all-encompassing news service and has been the number one network in cable for the last nine years and the most-watched television news channel for more than 23 consecutive years, currently attracting nearly 70% of the cable news viewing audience according to Nielsen Media Research. Notably, Nielsen/MRI Fusion has consistently shown FNC to be the network of choice for more Democrat and Independent viewers, with the most politically diverse audience in cable news. A 2024 Pew Research Center study found that more Americans named FNC as their main source for political news than any other network while a 2023 New York Times/Siena College poll found FNC as the leading single source of news for voters across the country. Owned by Fox Corporation, FNC is available in more than 60 million homes and dominates the cable news landscape, routinely notching the top 10 programs in the genre.

data.title: Many of us come to estate planning too late. Here's what finally pushed me to do it.
data.description:
By Beth Pinsker

If you recognize that you need a plan, don't let the moment slip away

The winter of 2015 was harsh in New York - snow, freezing rain, black ice - and I was afraid of slipping and hitting my head. This is why I got an estate plan.

This is not how most people go about this task, primarily because most people never go about this task at all. Only 30% to 40% of adults in the U.S. do any sort of estate planning.

But this is how it all jelled for me. I was penguin-walking home from work in my big boots and puffy coat, worrying what would happen if I fell and hurt myself in some awful, life-altering way - like my friend who fell and shattered her wrist in those storms. It was a worst-case scenario for me: My ex-husband would take over care of our young children, and, as they were the beneficiaries of my life insurance and the legal inheritors of my house and financial accounts, he'd be in control of all their money. I could no longer sleep at night.

You would think that more people would have moments in their lives like this and take care of essential to-do items - but what usually happens is that you have a fleeting, terrorizing thought like I did, and then it passes and you never get around to doing anything about it.

You might feel OK letting the moment go because you think you don't have enough assets to worry about, but wealthy people aren't taking care of estate planning, either. A recent study from Fidelity Charitable showed the stark reality that even among those who have a lot of assets to protect - high-net-worth people with more than $1 million in investable assets - only 66% had designated power of attorney and a mere 26% had made guardianship designations.

Looking back

I started thinking about that bad ice storm because I was recently pitched an interview with the person who got me started on my plan, Amanda Regnier (without the publicist realizing we had a deep history).

We've both come a long way since 2015, when Regnier made a house call as a fellow elementary-school parent to get me started in February of that year. She's now director of financial planning and senior wealth strategist at CIBC Private Wealth U.S., while I have a book coming out this fall based on estate-planning columns I wrote for MarketWatch called "My Mother's Money: A Guide to Financial Caregiving."

"Most of my clients came to me because they were worried about something happening to them," Regnier said when we did a video call to catch up. "Mostly it was mothers who would drive the conversation. They'd say, 'We just had this baby,' or 'I wasn't paying attention and now we have three kids, and, if something happened to us, I don't know what we'd do and who would get the kids.' "

In those long-ago days, her clients weren't extremely wealthy. The assets in question were more like $500,000 in life-insurance coverage and maybe a house. "They were mostly worried about who would be with the kids," she said. Now, with wealthier clients, there are other concerns, like setting up charitable giving and trusts for grandchildren, but people still mostly have concerns about who makes decisions if they get sick and who will care for their children if they can't.

"You have to make time, even if there's nobody standing there telling you to go do this," Regnier said. If going to a lawyer's office and plunking down a big check to talk about your own death sounds onerous, she noted that there are plenty of practicing estate lawyers who will do house calls like she did to get the ball rolling, making it friendly and comfortable.

The key task is to make an appointment with an attorney. You can ask around to find a professional - look on local lists or search online through organizations like the National Academy of Elder Law Attorneys. Regnier used to offer free seminars for local parents, and you might get an hour of a lawyer's time through a school auction or other charitable venue.

Once you have that booked, all you have to do is follow through, and you're likely to do that if it's on your calendar. You'll have to provide some information to the attorney, but it's not like you're going to be drafting legal documents yourself. Leave that to the professionals, and your main contribution will be signing the final papers with the proper witnesses. Doesn't that sound like something you can do?

Looking back, I realize I was about seven years late in all of this planning. Not unusual, but still late. A new Trust & Will survey found that only 19% of married couples create an estate plan within their first four years together, and 25% acted only after 10 years of marriage. I should have at least had a will in place when I was still married, or as soon as we had a baby, and certainly by the time the second child came along, in order to name a guardian. Or it should have been something I took care of as soon as we split up. Or when my father got sick the year before. But we all do the best we can.

Getting that estate package done, even with a delay, freed me up to plan proactively for the rest of my life. I got passionate about all sorts of planning, my kids grew up to be near-adults, and we have been living mostly happily ever after. This could be you, too, if you're ready to plan.

Got a question about investing, how it fits into your overall financial plan and what strategies can help you make the most out of your money? You can write to me at beth.pinsker@marketwatch.com. Please put "Fix My Portfolio" in the subject line.

You can also join the retirement conversation in our Facebook community: Retire Better with MarketWatch.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms, including via third parties.

More Fix My Portfolio

-- I transferred company stock out of an old 401(k). Now I'm worried I'm facing a nasty tax surprise.

-- Are bonds still a good retirement investment?

-- This Social Security question is so hard that even financial pros get stumped

-Beth Pinsker

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires
